action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 8 achieved a new best test spearman of 0.6735 using ESM2 regional embeddings (640-d) + 3 PLLD features with a linear model. Val_spr is 0.83, indicating a generalization gap to combinatorial test mutants. The training took ~16 min, but evaluation (predict.py running ESM2 on 50k test sequences) takes ~25 min. With ~1.94 hours left, I have budget for ~3 more iterations. Analysis: The current torch SGD-trained linear model may not be optimally regularized. sklearn's RidgeCV can find the exact optimal L2 regularization analytically in seconds, potentially improving generalization. I will train a RidgeCV model on the existing cached embeddings (v3.pt), try both full 643-dim features and PLLD-only (3-dim) features, and save the best. Predict.py will be updated to use the sklearn model if available, avoiding torch overhead during inference. This is a fast, high-ROI change that doesn't require recomputing ESM2 embeddings.
